config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan, pendapat, dan martabat orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankan individu seenaknya. Dari pengalaman saya melihat cara kerja masyarakat, keputusan yang benar justru biasanya mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan satu pihak sepenuhnya di atas yang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter a lot, but I do not accept the idea that they should automatically take priority over individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, social stability and collective goals are important, and I can understand why many people value them, especially when a country is trying to develop and avoid chaos. But I also think that if society is always placed above the individual, it can easily become a justification for ignoring personal dignity, fairness, and basic freedoms. The better principle is balance: society should protect the common good, but it should never use that as an excuse to trample individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, rasa aman, dan martabat pribadi justru harus jadi pagar utama. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi kalau hak individu tidak dilindungi, yang muncul bukan harmoni melainkan ketidakadilan yang diam-diam dinormalisasi. Bagi saya, prioritas yang benar adalah keseimbangan yang jelas: masyarakat kuat justru ketika individu tidak mudah dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank individual rights, because that rule is too easy to abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize stability, order, and collective goals, especially in a society that values social harmony and long-term development, but I do not think that makes individual rights secondary by default. When “the public interest” becomes a blanket excuse, it can hide unfairness and silence ordinary people, which in the long run weakens trust rather than strengthening society. A real strong society should protect both the common good and basic individual dignity, not sacrifice one for the other.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menutup-nutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa, dan itu sering membuat orang kecil yang paling dulu menanggung akibatnya. Saya juga tidak setuju dengan anggapan bahwa stabilitas otomatis lebih penting; stabilitas yang dibangun dengan menekan hak justru rapuh dan menyimpan ketidakadilan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang melindungi hak dasar dulu, lalu mengejar kepentingan bersama lewat aturan yang adil, bukan lewat pengorbanan individu secara serampangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, because those ideas are deeply tied to development and social harmony, but I have also seen how easily “the common good” can become a convenient excuse for ignoring ordinary people. If individual rights are treated as secondary, then fairness becomes fragile and the people with less power are the first to lose. A strong society should protect the common interest through just rules, not by assuming that personal dignity can always be sacrificed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
